author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22): cutover landed.

git-bug → jjforge cutover is complete. Commit `98f49d2`:
`docs/git-bug-cutover.md` (bridge doc with old → new id
mapping + how to read archived `refs/bugs/*`) and a full
CLAUDE.md rewrite (jjforge-first, new roadmap entry-point,
queries swapped to `jjf ls`).

Follow-up cleanup commit `edbaced`: the op-space merge
round (`adfc716`) had accidentally removed
`experiments/jj-cli-overhead/test-repo/a.txt` from the
index via jj's snapshot machinery. Restored, plus a
narrow `.gitignore` rule for `experiments/**/test-repo/.jj/`
so the same footgun doesn't return.

This makes `cli-replace-shim` genuinely ready. Per the
subagent: the shim's planner role is done; flipping it
removes the footgun where a future `bin/jjf new` would
land in the wrong tracker. Small ticket — file it under
this epic and dispatch when you're ready.

Migration trail for archival lookups:
- Decision: `git-bug bug show d12031c` (closed, option B
  recorded).
- Cutover plan + execution: `git-bug bug show 176f3c6`
  (closed, includes the full old→new id table in the
  closing comment).
- Cutover doc: `docs/git-bug-cutover.md`.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22): colocate-drift bug closed.

- `08cf14b` → closed. Commit `e8760b0`. New preflight
  `refuse_self_hosted_write` detects the jjforge source
  repo via marker-combo (`crates/jjf/Cargo.toml` +
  `docs/storage-format.md`) and refuses mutating verbs
  with kind `self_hosted_write_refused`. Bypass:
  `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` (loud in text mode, silent
  under `--json`). Reads pass through unmodified.

Tests: 185 workspace-wide (was 178). 7 new in
`crates/jjf/tests/self_host_guard.rs`.

CLAUDE.md "Operating in a colocated jj+git repo" section
rewritten with the corrected recovery recipe (`git
symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main && git reset --hard
main` — the old `jj edit main` advice was empirically
incomplete).

Per the bugs-before-features rule (now codified as step
2 of the orchestration loop), this unblocks `agent-ready`
(`69d5e1b`) which was the next feature ticket. The
orchestration loop can now safely run `jjf` from inside
this repo when `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` is set.

Closed-not-filed follow-ups (subagent surfaced, none
blocking): option B (full workspace isolation) as a
separate ticket if the bypass becomes friction-heavy;
a single `jjforge.toml` marker if the two-file
heuristic produces false-positives; cross-platform CI
attention on tests that use `std::env::temp_dir()`.
